note: Download progress bar LIES: snaps to ~85% (40-50GB) in <1s then crawls -> users think app is broken. RECURRING ~6 sessions, never fixed because docs/gotchas.md + builder doc call the 80% snap aria2c preallocation -do NOT fix-, so agents stop and circle. CORRECT framing: it is a progress-REPORTING bug not a throughput bug. aria2c preallocates / reports optimistic bytes; wrapper emits those as real progress; MPI-95 trusted the same wrong number. REAL FIX: report ACTUAL downloaded bytes (aria2c completedLength, not preallocated file size). Test: log completedLength vs os.path.getsize(.part) on a fresh Pod. Wrapper wrapper.py _download_aria2/emit_progress; app downloadManager.js _onRemoteInstallEvent. Must also CORRECT the misleading gotcha. NOT fixed tonight; deferred to a real test session.

KEY REGRESSION FACT (user): BEFORE aria2c was introduced, the progress bar was PROGRESSIVE (correct, real-byte). aria2c is the REGRESSION boundary. Pre-aria2 path = httpx streaming (aiter_bytes counts real bytes as they arrive -> progressive). aria2c swap -> preallocation + optimistic byte reporting -> instant 85% snap. So the FIX has a known-good REFERENCE: whatever the pre-aria2 httpx path reported. TWO FIX ROUTES to decide next session: (1) keep aria2c, fix reporting to use aria2c completedLength (true downloaded) instead of preallocated file size — preserves the 16-conn speed; (2) REVERT the download to the httpx path (still present as _download_httpx fallback) — restores the progressive bar AND was known to work. DECIDING QUESTION: WHY was aria2c added (gotcha says saturate-NIC/10-40x faster) — but live 2026-06-25 aria2c COLLAPSED to 1-35MB/s on the tail anyway, so it may NOT be delivering the speed that justified it. If aria2c is not actually faster end-to-end, the httpx revert is strictly better (progressive bar, simpler, no preallocation lie). Action: benchmark aria2c vs httpx end-to-end on a fresh Pod from R2 (total wall-clock for the 41GB), THEN choose fix route. git-blame the wrapper for the aria2c commit to recover the original httpx progress code as the reference.

DIAGNOSTIC (user, confirmed): the 85%-snap progress behavior is IDENTICAL on network volume AND ephemeral (local NVMe) disk. This RULES OUT the disk-write-flush theory as the cause of the PROGRESS LIE — ephemeral local NVMe is fast, yet the bar still snaps to 85% instantly. Therefore the false 85% is PURELY aria2c preallocation / optimistic byte reporting, independent of disk speed. Narrows the fix to: wrapper must report aria2c completedLength (true downloaded bytes) instead of preallocated file size / os.path.getsize(.part). NOTE this also weakens MPI-139s throughput/disk-wall angle as the PROGRESS cause (the tail crawl may still be partly disk on volume, but the BAR LIE is not disk). Deferred to post-release by user decision (avoid new download-path code on release night).

summary: Progress-bar lie RESOLVED by R2 (HF pathology); 9 commits fix denominator snap + 8 adjacent download bugs; verifying restored, pause-hidden-on-remote, disk-full crash, CPU image tag. Live-verified local+remote.
Archived completed card during board cleanup.
